---
title: "Metabolites Analysis"
output: html_document
date: "2026-02-25"
---

```{r}
# Install if needed
if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
    install.packages("BiocManager")
BiocManager::install("zellkonverter")

library(zellkonverter)
```

```{r}
# Load the file
load("~/Desktop/iawards/NMR metabolomics/NMR_peakarea_metadata.RData")

# Check what objects were loaded
ls()

# Explore the main object
class(NMR_peakarea_metadata)
dim(NMR_peakarea_metadata)

# Preview rows and columns
head(NMR_peakarea_metadata)
colnames(NMR_peakarea_metadata)
rownames(NMR_peakarea_metadata)

# Summary statistics
summary(NMR_peakarea_metadata)

# Check for missing values
sum(is.na(NMR_peakarea_metadata))


# The sample IDs are column 1 instead of row names, so changing this
# Set it as row names
# Check the name of the first column
colnames(NMR_peakarea_metadata)[1]
rownames(NMR_peakarea_metadata) <- NMR_peakarea_metadata[, 1]
NMR_peakarea_metadata <- NMR_peakarea_metadata[, -1]  # drop the column after

# Verify
dim(NMR_peakarea_metadata)
rownames(NMR_peakarea_metadata)

# Are all columns numeric?
all(sapply(NMR_peakarea_metadata, is.numeric))

# Check for missing values
sum(is.na(NMR_peakarea_metadata))

# Get a sense of the value distributions
summary(NMR_peakarea_metadata)

# Check variance across metabolites - low variance features may not be useful
metabolite_vars <- apply(NMR_peakarea_metadata, 2, var)
sort(metabolite_vars)[1:10]  # lowest variance metabolites
hist(metabolite_vars, main = "Metabolite Variance Distribution")

```

The metabolite columns are being treated as non-numeric because they have quotes embedded in the titles so R reads the values as characters instead of numbers. So i will remove the quotes from the titles of the metabolites.

```{r}
# Check the column names to see where metabolites start and end
colnames(NMR_peakarea_metadata)[1:38]      
colnames(NMR_peakarea_metadata)[39:109]    

ncol(NMR_peakarea_metadata)  # total number of columns
colnames(NMR_peakarea_metadata)[107:ncol(NMR_peakarea_metadata)]  # check the end

metabolite_cols <- colnames(NMR_peakarea_metadata)[1:38]
metadata_cols <- colnames(NMR_peakarea_metadata)[39:122]

# Convert metabolites to numeric
NMR_peakarea_metadata[, metabolite_cols] <- lapply(
  NMR_peakarea_metadata[, metabolite_cols],
  function(x) as.numeric(gsub("'", "", x))
)

# Verify
all(sapply(NMR_peakarea_metadata[, metabolite_cols], is.numeric))
sum(is.na(NMR_peakarea_metadata[, metabolite_cols]))

# Split into two clean objects
metabolite_matrix <- NMR_peakarea_metadata[, metabolite_cols]
sample_metadata <- NMR_peakarea_metadata[, metadata_cols]

dim(metabolite_matrix)  # should be 32 x 38
dim(sample_metadata)    # should be 32 x 84
```

Looking at S. aureus levels

```{r}
# Check the s aureus column
table(sample_metadata$s_aureus_culture_growth_viapath, useNA = "always")

# Also look at lesional status
table(sample_metadata$lesional, sample_metadata$s_aureus_culture_growth_viapath, useNA = "always")
```

Converting growth level (none\<light\<moderate\<heavy) to an ordered factor so R knows that there is ranking between levels (useful for downstream stats, comparisons and plotting).

```{r}
# Convert to ordered factor
sample_metadata$s_aureus_culture_growth_viapath <- factor(
  sample_metadata$s_aureus_culture_growth_viapath,
  levels = c("none", "light", "moderate", "heavy"),
  ordered = TRUE
)

# Verify
levels(sample_metadata$s_aureus_culture_growth_viapath)
```

Looking at S. aureus association to different metabolites

Using Spearman's rank: correlation -1 to +1

```{r}
# Convert ordered factor to numeric for correlation (none=1, light=2, moderate=3, heavy=4)
s_aureus_numeric <- as.numeric(sample_metadata$s_aureus_culture_growth_viapath)

# Correlate with each metabolite
cor_results <- cor(s_aureus_numeric, metabolite_matrix, method = "spearman")

# Tidy up and sort by absolute correlation
cor_df <- data.frame(
  metabolite = colnames(metabolite_matrix),
  correlation = as.numeric(cor_results)
)
cor_df <- cor_df[order(abs(cor_df$correlation), decreasing = TRUE), ]

# View top results
head(cor_df, 10)

# Get correlation and p-value for each metabolite
cor_pvals <- sapply(metabolite_matrix, function(x) {
  test <- cor.test(s_aureus_numeric, x, method = "spearman")
  c(correlation = test$estimate, p_value = test$p.value)
})

cor_df2 <- as.data.frame(t(cor_pvals))
cor_df2$metabolite <- rownames(cor_df2)
cor_df2$p_adj <- p.adjust(cor_df2$p_value, method = "BH")  # Benjamini-Hochberg correction
cor_df2 <- cor_df2[order(abs(cor_df2$correlation), decreasing = TRUE), ]

head(cor_df2, 10)


############## Visualisations ##########
# Table of all metabolites and p values
cor_df2 <- cor_df2[order(abs(cor_df2$correlation), decreasing = TRUE), ]
print(cor_df2, n = 38)
write.csv(cor_df2, "~/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/MetabolitesxSamples/s_aureus_metabolite_correlations.csv", row.names = FALSE)


##### Boxplot
install.packages("ggpubr")
library(ggpubr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)

# Select top 6 metabolites by adjusted p-value
top_metabolites <- cor_df2 %>%
  arrange(p_adj) %>%
  slice_head(n = 6) %>%
  pull(metabolite)

top_metabolites  # check which ones were selected

# Combine with S. aureus status
plot_data <- metabolite_matrix[, top_metabolites]
plot_data$s_aureus <- sample_metadata$s_aureus_culture_growth_viapath

# Reshape to long format
plot_long <- pivot_longer(plot_data, cols = -s_aureus, 
                          names_to = "metabolite", values_to = "level")

# Plot
ggplot(plot_long, aes(x = s_aureus, y = level, fill = s_aureus)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ metabolite, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = "S. aureus burden", y = "Peak area", 
       title = "Metabolite levels by S. aureus burden") +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = list(c("none", "heavy"), 
                                        c("light", "heavy"),
                                        c("moderate", "heavy")),
                     method = "wilcox.test",
                     label = "p.signif") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
ggsave("~/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/MetabolitesxSamples/s_aureus_boxplots.png", width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)


### heatmap
library(pheatmap)

# Scale metabolite matrix
scaled_metabolites <- scale(metabolite_matrix)

# Create annotation for samples
annotation <- data.frame(
  s_aureus = sample_metadata$s_aureus_culture_growth_viapath,
  lesional = sample_metadata$lesional,
  row.names = rownames(metabolite_matrix)
)

# Plot heatmap
annotation_colours <- list(
  s_aureus = c(none = "lightyellow", light = "yellow", moderate = "orange", heavy = "red"),
  lesional = c(LES = "darkred", NONLES = "grey80")
)

png("~/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/MetabolitesxSamples/s_aureus_heatmap.png", width = 10, height = 8, units = "in", res = 300)
pheatmap(t(scaled_metabolites),
         annotation_col = annotation,
         annotation_colors = annotation_colours,
         show_colnames = FALSE,
         clustering_method = "ward.D2",
         main = "Metabolite levels across samples",
         fontsize_row = 8)
dev.off()

#### Heatmap of just significant metaboloites 
# Get significant metabolites
sig_metabolites <- cor_df2 %>%
  filter(p_adj < 0.05) %>%
  pull(metabolite)

sig_metabolites  # check how many there are
png("~/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/MetabolitesxSamples/sig_s_aureus_heatmap.png", width = 10, height = 8, units = "in", res = 300)
pheatmap(t(scaled_metabolites[, sig_metabolites]),
         annotation_col = annotation,
         annotation_colors = annotation_colours,
         show_colnames = FALSE,
         clustering_method = "ward.D2",
         main = "Significant metabolites by S. aureus burden",
         fontsize_row = 8)
dev.off()

# Check correlation between lesional status and s aureus
table(sample_metadata$lesional, sample_metadata$s_aureus_culture_growth_viapath)

ggplot(cor_df2, aes(x = reorder(metabolite, correlation.rho), 
                    y = correlation.rho, 
                    fill = p_adj < 0.05)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "steelblue", "FALSE" = "grey70"),
                    labels = c("TRUE" = "p_adj < 0.05", "FALSE" = "ns"),
                    name = "Significance") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = "Metabolite", 
       y = "Spearman correlation", 
       title = "Metabolite correlations with S. aureus burden") +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8))

ggsave("~/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/MetabolitesxSamples/s_aureus_correlation_barplot.png", width = 8, height = 8, dpi = 300)



###### Look at metabolite associations with lesinal status
# Convert lesional to numeric
lesional_numeric <- ifelse(sample_metadata$lesional == "LES", 1, 0)

# Correlate with each metabolite
lesional_cor <- sapply(metabolite_matrix, function(x) {
  test <- cor.test(lesional_numeric, x, method = "spearman")
  c(correlation = test$estimate, p_value = test$p.value)
})

lesional_df <- as.data.frame(t(lesional_cor))
lesional_df$metabolite <- rownames(lesional_df)
lesional_df$p_adj <- p.adjust(lesional_df$p_value, method = "BH")
lesional_df <- lesional_df[order(abs(lesional_df$correlation), decreasing = TRUE), ]

head(lesional_df, 10)

```

SA status and lesional status both linked to metabolite level?

Test each metabolite with both lesional and sa status as predictors

```{r}
# Test each metabolite with both lesional and s_aureus as predictors
multi_results <- lapply(metabolite_cols, function(met) {
  df <- data.frame(
    metabolite = metabolite_matrix[[met]],
    lesional = lesional_numeric,
    s_aureus = s_aureus_numeric
  )
  model <- lm(metabolite ~ lesional + s_aureus, data = df)
  coefs <- summary(model)$coefficients
  data.frame(
    metabolite = met,
    lesional_p = coefs["lesional", "Pr(>|t|)"],
    s_aureus_p = coefs["s_aureus", "Pr(>|t|)"]
  )
})

multi_df <- do.call(rbind, multi_results)
multi_df$lesional_p_adj <- p.adjust(multi_df$lesional_p, method = "BH")
multi_df$s_aureus_p_adj <- p.adjust(multi_df$s_aureus_p, method = "BH")


# View metabolites significant for s_aureus after controlling for lesional
# Ordered by most significant
multi_df[multi_df$s_aureus_p_adj < 0.05, ] %>%
  arrange(s_aureus_p_adj)
```

Update the visualisations based on the most significant metabolites when accounting for lesionality.

```{r}
############### boxplot
# Get the 8 significant metabolites in order
sig_multi_metabolites <- multi_df[multi_df$s_aureus_p_adj < 0.05, ] %>%
  arrange(s_aureus_p_adj) %>%
  pull(metabolite)

# Plot
plot_data <- metabolite_matrix[, sig_multi_metabolites]
plot_data$s_aureus <- sample_metadata$s_aureus_culture_growth_viapath

plot_long <- pivot_longer(plot_data, cols = -s_aureus,
                          names_to = "metabolite", values_to = "level")

# Keep metabolites in significance order
plot_long$metabolite <- factor(plot_long$metabolite, levels = sig_multi_metabolites)

ggplot(plot_long, aes(x = s_aureus, y = level, fill = s_aureus)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ metabolite, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = "S. aureus burden", y = "Peak area",
       title = "Metabolite levels by S. aureus burden",
       subtitle = "Significant after controlling for lesional status") +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = list(c("none", "heavy"),
                                        c("light", "heavy"),
                                        c("moderate", "heavy")),
                     method = "wilcox.test",
                     label = "p.signif") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

ggsave("~/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/MetabolitesxSamples/s_aureus_boxplots_updated.png", width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)

######### Heatmap
png("~/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/MetabolitesxSamples/s_aureus_heatmap_updated.png", width = 10, height = 8, units = "in", res = 300)
pheatmap(t(scaled_metabolites[, sig_multi_metabolites]),
         annotation_col = annotation,
         annotation_colors = annotation_colours,
         show_colnames = FALSE,
         clustering_method = "ward.D2",
         main = "Metabolites significantly associated with S. aureus burden\n(independent of lesional status)",
         fontsize_row = 10)
dev.off()


######### Correlation barplot
# Merge s_aureus correlations with multivariate significance
cor_df2$independent_sig <- cor_df2$metabolite %in% sig_multi_metabolites

ggplot(cor_df2, aes(x = reorder(metabolite, correlation.rho),
                    y = correlation.rho,
                    fill = independent_sig)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "steelblue", "FALSE" = "grey70"),
                    labels = c("TRUE" = "Independent of lesional status", 
                               "FALSE" = "Not significant"),
                    name = "") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = "Metabolite",
       y = "Spearman correlation with S. aureus burden",
       title = "Metabolite correlations with S. aureus burden") +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
        legend.position = "bottom")

ggsave("~/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/MetabolitesxSamples/s_aureus_correlation_barplot_updated.png", width = 8, height = 8, dpi = 300)
```
